figures.caption: Table 1: Comparison of geolocation accuracy under different error thresholds on Im2GPS 3k, EarthWhere, and GeoRC. Best results are in bold.

Figure 1: The evolution of global visual geo-localization paradigms. Unlike traditional feature-based matching and static LLM reasoning, our proposed GeoSkill utilizes an evolving Skill-Graph to iteratively refine geographic knowledge through autonomous feedback loops.

Figure 2: The overall framework of GeoSkill. The system follows a four-step workflow: (1) Offline Skill Library Initialization, which distills human heuristics into a reusable skill library; (2) Online Inference and Rollout, where the LVLM agent retrieves relevant skills and dynamically constructs a task-specific Skill-Graph; (3) Offline Logging and Validation, which records reasoning trajectories and verifies them with external tools; and (4) Offline Skill & Graph Updating, which refines and expands the skill library based on outcome feedback.
